title: PONY AI Inc. to Report Second Quarter and Interim Financial Results for 2026 on August 18, 2026
description: NEW YORK, July 17, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Pony AI Inc. (“Pony.ai” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: PONY; HKEX: 2026), a global leader in achieving large-scale mass production and commercialization of autonomous driving technology, today announced that it will report its unaudited financial results for the second quarter 2026 and for the six months ended June 30, 2026 before the U.S. market opens on Tuesday, August 18, 2026.

title: Russian troops survive just 20-30 minutes on Ukraine’s front lines, CIA director says
description: Russian recruits arriving on the battlefield in Ukraine survive an estimated 20 to 30 minutes before they are killed or wounded, the CIA director said.

title: Saab to supply sensors and combat systems for new German warships after F126 scrapped
description: Swedish defense firm Saab has secured an 8.7 billion kronor ($900 million) contract to supply combat systems and sensors for the German Navy’s MEKO ships.

title: Franco-German defense cooperation under strain as Macron, Merz meet
description: The meetings come weeks after industrial rivalries forced Paris and Berlin to scrap the landmark Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter jet program.

title: Fedorov out as Ukraine’s defense minister in major government shake-up
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Mykhailo Fedorov as the country's minister of defense after just six months in office.

title: US Air Force turns to cheaper cruise missiles it can buy by the thousands
description: The U.S. Air Force is turning to cruise missiles it can buy by the thousand, cheap enough to shoot many for less than it once cost to shoot one.

title: MBDA appoints Airbus executive Jean-Brice Dumont as new CEO
description: MBDA, Europe’s largest missile maker, appointed Airbus Defence and Space executive Jean-Brice Dumont as its new chief executive officer effective Nov. 1.

title: First the coast guard, then an ICBM: China tests new, long-term ways to hold off rivals in Asia
description: Experts say Beijing used both methods to try out new, attention-grabbing and long-term ways to deter others from challenging its territorial claims.

title: Pressure mounts on US defense executives to boost weapons production
description: Defense executives face pressure to boost production as prolonged conflicts have consumed large quantities of missiles, interceptors and other weapons.

description: The U.S. conducted a new wave of strikes against Iranian military sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of the Islamic Republic's ports.

title: The US Navy is eyeing next-gen carrier-based drones. Here’s what they might do.

title: Manufacturing woes hamper US 155-mm ammo production
description: Manufacturing problems are dashing the U.S. Army’s plans to boost production of urgently needed 155-mm howitzer shells, a government watchdog said.
